meta.title: Katsurazawa Kokusetsu Ski Resort
meta.description: ~60 minutes from Sapporo—national ski hill with kids park and snow tubes. Adult day ticket ¥2,410; senior & elementary ¥1,360.

hero.title:
Katsurazawa
Kokusetsu Ski
hero.tagline: Snow tubes and premium JAPOW—an uncrowded national hill in Mikasa.

lift.label: Lift
lift.note: 1 pair lift 811 m · virtually no wait

transit.title:
~60 minutes from Sapporo
to Mikasa's national hill.
transit.lead: About 60 minutes from central Sapporo via the Doto Expressway and Mikasa IC—and about 60 minutes from New Chitose Airport. A local hill Sapporo-area families visit on weekends, skipping Teine and Sapporo Kokusai crowds. 70 ha of wide terrain with virtually no lift lines.
transit.imgAlt: Winter drive from the Sapporo area to Katsurazawa Kokusetsu Ski Resort in Mikasa

primary.title: 31° wide slope & long-lasting JAPOW
primary.lead: North-facing terrain keeps dry, fluffy powder in shape—one wide 811 m pair-lift slope at max 31° and avg 16°, loved by carving fans. Even on holiday midweeks, virtually no lift lines at this hidden Mikasa gem (LP concept).
primary.imgAlt: Wide powder slope at Katsurazawa Kokusetsu Ski Resort

secondary.title: Kids park & snow tubes
secondary.lead: The city of Mikasa's family hook for the Sapporo belt—tube slides and beginner runs in a compact 70 ha hill that closes at 16:00, ideal for same-day trips.

pass.title:
Adults ¥2,410,
senior & elementary ¥1,360.
pass.lead: National-hill pricing at less than one-third of major resorts—adult day ¥2,410, junior high ¥1,780, senior (60+) and elementary ¥1,360. Season pass ¥15,000 for locals while welcoming Sapporo-belt family day trips (LP concept).
pass.noticeBody: Lifts may stop when wind exceeds 6 m/s—check the official site or TEL 01267-6-8235. Hours 9:00–16:00 (LP concept).
pass.imgAlt: ~60 minutes from Sapporo—value pricing at a national hill

rental.title:
Ski & snowboard sets
¥3,500 each—travel light.
rental.lead: Ski set and snowboard set ¥3,500 each—Sapporo day-trip families can gear up on site and start runs immediately. Rest and lodge meals at Yama-no-Ie and Center House.
rental.whisper: Hands-free arrival plus a 60-minute Sapporo drive minimizes friction for weekend family snow days (LP strategy).

yukinko.title:
After 16:00,
Mikasa onsen awaits.
yukinko.lead: The hill closes at 16:00—5–10 minutes by car to Yunomoto Onsen Ryokan (day bath ¥600) or Taiko-no-Yu at Michi-no-Eki Mikasa on the way back to Sapporo. Lift-ticket discounts may apply (check locally).
yukinko.whisper: TEL 01267-6-8235 (Katsurazawa Kokusetsu). Confirm kids park and tube status on official channels before visiting.
yukinko.imgAlt: Yunomoto Onsen in Mikasa—day-trip bath after skiing

family.title:
Snow tubes &
first snow for families.
family.lead: Kids park tubes, four beginner-to-intermediate courses, and Lake Katsurazawa smelt fishing nearby—morning ski, afternoon lake, evening onsen: a full Mikasa winter day (LP concept). Family Land Mikasa amusement park is ~5 minutes on foot.
family.noticeBody: Weekend family traffic from the Sapporo belt—tube and park hours change by season; confirm by phone or official info before visiting (LP concept).
family.imgAlt: Families on snow tubes at the kids park

workation.title:
Leave Sapporo in the morning,
snow by late morning.
workation.lead: A classic Sapporo family day trip—~60 minutes via Mikasa IC. Morning kids park, lunch at Yama-no-Ie (karaage set), afternoon at Lake Katsurazawa—a Mikasa loop pairing local food and onsen (LP concept).

03.title: Lunch at Yama-no-Ie
03.body: Popular karaage set and katsu sandwich

05.title: Yunomoto Onsen
05.body: Aigamo hot pot and day bath homeward

local.title: Sapporo belt × national hill guide
local.body: Katsurazawa as a crowd-free alternative to Teine and Sapporo Kokusai—make the physical assets visible: 70 ha, ¥2,410, no lift lines, via kids-park CMS and weekend LP (report ch. 5).

schools.title: Mikasa winter gourmet stamp rally
schools.body: Cross-promote Yama-no-Ie lodge food, Takashimaya cafeteria jumbo zangi, Yunomoto aigamo nabe, and Manpuku nanko—raising stay time and spend in Mikasa city (report ch. 5).

summer.title: Year-round loop: lake & amusement park
summer.body: Bridge summer campers at adjacent Family Land Mikasa to winter skiing—integrating green-season and winter customer bases (report ch. 6).

access.note: ~60 min from central Sapporo · ~60 min from New Chitose Airport · via Mikasa IC
